(probing)How would you represent Michael's age (x) when he was 1 year younger as an equation?
turns.learner_turn: The equation would be x-1 = the age of Michael when he was 1 year younger.

turns.tutor_turn: (focus)Correct. How would you represent twice that age (x-1) in an equation?
turns.learner_turn: The equation would be 2(x-1) = twice the age of Michael when he was 1 year younger.

turns.tutor_turn: (probing)Now how would you represent 1 year older than 2(x-1) as an equation?
turns.learner_turn: The equation would be 2(x-1) + 1 = 1 year older than twice the age of Michael when he was 1 year younger.
